# tokens: # object — nominal type with fields, inheritance, and instantiation
# `maketype Object {field:Type …}` builds an object type — AQL's
# inheritance-aware type form. Object types can be:
#   - anonymous       — `maketype Object {a:String}` produces an unnamed
#                       Type with an internal ID (Object/T_…).
#   - named           — `def Foo (maketype Object {a:String})` installs Foo
#                       on the type stack.
#   - inheriting      — `def Bar (maketype Foo {b:Integer})` extends
#                       Foo with new fields. The result type ID is
#                       `Object/Foo/Bar` and the instance carries
#                       both Foo's and Bar's field constraints.
# Instantiation goes via `make NAME data`. The result is a typed
# object value that:
#   - renders as `Object/<path>{k:v,…}`,
#   - reports `typeof` as the named type,
#   - supports dotted field access (`.field`),
#   - satisfies parent-type `is` checks.
# Field validation: `make` checks that `data` carries every required
# field with values that satisfy each declared type. Missing fields
# raise an error.
# Runs against the production lang registry (test/go/langspec).
# === Test fixtures (q-suffixed spec-runner words) ===
#   addq    — [Number Number] forward-prec — args[1] + args[0]
#   lengthq — [List] forward-prec — list length
# === end fixtures ===
# 1. Anonymous object types
# `object {fields}` produces an Type with a synthesized
# internal name (Object/T_…). The exact ID is non-deterministic, so
# this section relies on type-stable assertions (typeof, is) rather
# than literal string comparison.
# An anonymous Type reports its `typeof` as a synthesised name
# (Object/T_xxx) — the internal ID used to disambiguate anonymous
# instances. Tests can't pin the exact ID, so this section uses
# named types in subsequent rows. Bare construction succeeds:
lengthq [ maketype Object { a:Integer } ]
note: the constructor produces exactly one stack value

# tokens: lengthq [ maketype Object { a:String b:Boolean } ]
note: multi-field anonymous still produces one value

# tokens: lengthq [ maketype Object { } ]
# 2. Named object types via `def NAME (maketype Object {…})`
# `def NAME (maketype Object …)` binds the named object type; once bound
# the bare name resolves to the Type value at that name.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) typeof Foo
note: named Type reports its own name

# tokens: def Foo (maketype Object { a:String b:Boolean }) typeof Foo
# 3. Instantiation via `make`
# `make NAME data` validates each declared field against `data` and
# returns a typed object value.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) make Foo { a:'hi' } typeof
note: the made value's typeof is the type name

# tokens: def Foo (maketype Object { a:String b:Integer }) make Foo { a:'hi' b:9 } typeof
# Missing required field → error.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) make Foo { }
# 4. Field access via dotted accessor
# Object instances support the same `.field` syntax that maps do.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) make Foo { a:'hi' } .a
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String b:Integer }) make Foo { a:'x' b:7 } .a
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String b:Integer }) make Foo { a:'x' b:7 } .b
# Chained field access — object whose field is another object.
def Inner (maketype Object { v:Integer }) def Outer (maketype Object { i:Inner }) make Outer { i:( make Inner { v:99 } ) } .i .v
# 5. Inheritance
# `def Child (maketype Parent {fields})` makes Child inherit from
# Parent. The full type path is `Object/Parent/Child` and Child
# instances carry both sets of fields.
# Path / structure inspection — typeof reports the most-specific name.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) def Bar (maketype Foo { b:Integer }) typeof Bar
# A Bar instance satisfies Foo (parent) via `is`.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) def Bar (maketype Foo { b:Integer }) make Bar { a:'x' b:9 } is Foo
# Multi-level inheritance.
def A (maketype Object { a:Integer }) def B (maketype A { b:Integer }) def C (maketype B { c:Integer }) make C { a:1 b:2 c:3 } is A
def A (maketype Object { a:Integer }) def B (maketype A { b:Integer }) def C (maketype B { c:Integer }) make C { a:1 b:2 c:3 } typeof
# Inherited field access — Foo's `a` is reachable on a Bar instance.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) def Bar (maketype Foo { b:Integer }) make Bar { a:'x' b:9 } .a
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) def Bar (maketype Foo { b:Integer }) make Bar { a:'x' b:9 } .b
# 6. Composition with def
# A made instance can be captured by `def`. The bound name then
# substitutes the instance value at every use site.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:String }) def f make Foo { a:'hi' } f .a
note: def-captured object instance preserves type & fields

# tokens: def Foo (maketype Object { a:Integer }) def f make Foo { a:42 } f .a addq 8
# 7. Multiple instances
# Each `make` call yields a fresh object value; they're independent.
def Foo (maketype Object { a:Integer }) def x make Foo { a:1 } def y make Foo { a:2 } ( x .a ) addq ( y .a )
